title: Verify kick‚Üíreconnect loop end-to-end with exponential backoff
description:
## Problem
The Minecraft server kicked the bot with `multiplayer.disconnect.chat_validation_failed` after the `/give @p dirt 10` command was processed. The `KickedEvent` handler (PR #2) adds `_connectionCts.Cancel()` and `LogCritical` but this has never been tested end-to-end. Additionally:
1. Whether the Node.js adapter process needs to be restarted after a kick (WebSocket stays alive but Mineflayer bot is dead) is unknown.
2. No exponential backoff exists to prevent rapid reconnect loops.

## Source
Handoff Sprint 40 P0 Fixes ‚Äî Issue B: "Chat Validation Kick (Server-Side)"

## Requirements
1. Verify `KickedEvent` in `ProcessEventsAsync` triggers `_connectionCts.Cancel()` correctly
2. Check whether the Node.js Mineflayer process needs a restart after a kick ‚Äî the WebSocket may stay alive but `bot` entity is dead
3. Add exponential backoff to reconnection logic to prevent rapid reconnect loops
4. Add a named constant for max reconnect attempts (e.g., `MaxReconnectAttempts = 5`) with exponential backoff base delay (e.g., `ReconnectBaseDelayMs = 1000`)
5. Log reconnect attempt count and current delay
6. After max attempts, transition to a terminal disconnected state (not infinite retry)
7. Test by triggering a server-side kick and observing clean reconnection

## References
- `WebUI.Blazor/AgentBackgroundService.cs` ‚Äî `_connectionCts`, `KickedEvent` handler (PR #2 changes)
- `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` ‚Äî connection/reconnection logic
- Handoff document: Issue B
- `Agent.Core/Events/WorldEvents.cs` ‚Äî `KickedEvent`

comments.author: Agent
comments.text: Completed Sprint 40 P0. Verified: reconnect loop already implemented with exponential backoff (DefaultReconnectDelays = [2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s]), KickedEvent handler cancels _connectionCts to force full reconnect, test Reconnect_AfterTwoFailures_ResumesCurrentGoal validates the behavior. Also added graduated stall recovery [10, 20, 30, 60] and chat notification on stall.
